_note: PRE-KEEP-WARM laptop sample (generated against a mockserver-snapshot image BEFORE core commit 142cbc778; the simple-GET reqs/conn collapses 235->3.4 = churn). ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY — the chart renderer reads the LIVE CI artifact, not this fixture; replace with a fresh keep-warm CI run (which should hold reqs/conn high across the ladder). laptop-scale validation (fat injector 8-13 + WIDE Envoy 2-7/conc6 on a 14-core contended Mac, short 14s holds). Demonstrates the NEW connection-reuse assertion: offered=2000 reqs/conn=235 (clean reuse, 26% CPU) is the trusted ceiling; offered>=6000 reqs/conn drops to ~3 (the forward client's idle-only pool cannot reuse sub-ms requests fast enough = CHURN) and is flagged NOT trustworthy, so the ceiling is honest (no churn-corrupted number). Absolute rps is laptop-noisy. Controlled fixed-2000rps: simple=26% vs complex=41% CPU => simple rps/core (~7700) > complex rps/core (~4900), i.e. complex costs ~1.6x CPU/request (expected direction). CI's idle box produces the real plateau.
